title: Arani Korden

{{Quote|One little firefight and you get so testy!|[[Deel Surool]] to Arani Korden|<ref name="Battle for Theed">"[[Battle for Theed]]"</ref>}}
'''Arani Korden''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was the daughter of a [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] noble and later served as [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] to represent [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
[[File:Arani_Korden-young.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Young Arani]]
Arani was always in pursuit of adventure and excitement, and had a habit of getting herself into trouble. She was sneaky and confident, and had a great sense of humor. She attended the Theed [[Royal House of Learning/Legends|Royal House of Learning]].<ref name="SP">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sketchGallery_1|text=Sneak Preview: ''Star Wars Invasion of Theed Adventure Game''}}</ref> Korden, [[Galak]], [[Rorworr (Naboo)|Rorworr]], and [[Rann I-Kanu]] were tasked by Rann's master to seek out a rumored group of smugglers hiding in a base near Theed. One of Arani's contacts came up with the location of the group after a couple of days. There, they found a hidden smuggler's base led by [[Saidle Frex]]. They defeat Frex and his gang and rescued the abducted archeology professor [[Tasrah Boh]] in the process.<ref name="SoN">{{GamerCite|2|The Predators}} and {{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=rpg_fastPlay_intro|text=The Smugglers of Naboo|int=The Smugglers of Naboo}}</ref>

At the age of 16, she played a role in defending Naboo from [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] forces in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] as a member of the [[Naboo Underground]],<ref name="IoTAG">[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]]</ref> and the [[Naboo Resistance]].<ref name="FF91">{{FactFile|91|TRA 17-18|The Trade Federation Attack}}</ref> A decade later, Arani found herself in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], watching a holographic representation of a battle, while [[Kelko]] surveyed the action with a pair of [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]].<ref name="CWCG">''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]''</ref> After the fall of the Republic, she and Kelko were seen on an Imperial city, trying to distract an Imperial while her [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] companion tried to get away.<ref name="TFUCG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

During the reign of the Empire, Arani's previous experience with Jedi with the Naboo underground and the academy lead her to help a number of Jedi escape the initial portions of the Jedi purge. Her time with the Naboo Resistance also lead to her relative, [[Tavin Korden|Tavin]], to place her in charge of the security of the family business: [[Korden Outfitting and Surveying]]. Arani used her position during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] to allow the Rebellion to steal needed supplies from warehouses.<ref name="GOI">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]'', House Korden</ref>

After becoming a [[Battle of Skorrupon|hero of Skorrupon]], Arani returned to her homeworld,<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Naboo,<ref name="IoTAG" /> and became the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] senator of the planet.<ref name="GCG" />

In later years, Arani became the CEO of the Korden Outfitting and Surveying, and continued to use its resources to aid those fighting oppression. As the New Republic began to fall into a dictatorship, Arani used the company to found out-of-the-way settlements in which she let those who needed to keep a low profile escape in to.<ref name="GOI" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Arani Korden}}
In the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' rulebook, under the 'Classes' section, this character is called "Arani Karden". Though class-wise, Arani Karden is a noble, whereas Arani Korden is a "scoundrel", and somewhat younger. This misspelled name is corrected in the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Saga Edition Core Rulebook]]'', though Korden is still classed as a noble.
